summary: labeler.test.ts provides solid unit test coverage of core labeler logic with good edge case handling (empty files, path normalization, priority ordering). However, the suite is severely incomplete: (1) No direct tests of index.ts CLI functionality—no tests for argument parsing, format flags (json vs lines), stdin input, or error handling; (2) workflow.test.ts attempts E2E testing via act but provides only 3 happy-path cases with no error scenarios for malformed JSON, missing arguments, or invalid patterns; (3) setupRepo silently skips files if they don't exist rather than validating all dependencies; (4) extractLabels relies on fragile regex parsing of output format not shown in the implementation; (5) Test assumes .github/workflows/pr-label-assigner.yml, rules.json, and package.json exist but these are external dependencies. Suite would fail to catch regressions in error handling, CLI parsing, or output formatting. Violates requirement that "every single test case must execute through the GitHub Actions workflow" when only 3 cases are provided.
